textByHashes.afd75c75c462af61088be625fb27cfab37c78e8c: Hello, to what do I owe this pleasure?
textByHashes.0fa16280450e442d2323192639f1fbfc5aa7504e: So you're supposed to be Robert the Strong?
textByHashes.444779636ff784b1963a50ac965795f53e43fe5d: Are you sure this is a good idea?
textByHashes.a77512d33834cabc738d5c65c1323b8d96762009: I said left at the camel!
textByHashes.b8ad27f42f6c65b4ad19f4ad91ac4ce4163a73ef: You're too close to the pyramid!
textByHashes.fa8f6a5b3d4c200f829b55ff53c7fa58ae6de442: He's meant to be with you. You haven't lost him have you?
textByHashes.0ef163291d1a2b34eddd2d4ec0510a27a66f582f: Well keep an eye on him for me.
textByHashes.35dcab3b966ec44ba445b67a19aa4a70dca0f772: Hmm... Nice to meet you, . I'm Neite.
textByHashes.45994690c28973b2b8e09c3a8664c7cc774db266: Oh there's far more than just me. The others are just shy. We do also get visitors from outside the city sometimes, like that Bob. He's quite a handsome beast...
textByHashes.f65a3a34b1def837cf5859d93b04caa2a0fb9456: Well, life here in the desert can be a lonely existence, even with all my busy work... Still, to be honest, he worries me.
textByHashes.3397c7a8b9eb593b292ea8e4344ac2bcbb5a587e: Well, I pride myself on my knowledge of the family lines of cats. Obviously, Bob features quite heavily in most family trees, but he himself has none!
textByHashes.57cf40628f2ae0ed0112faf224af5a69ffeafaeb: Nobody is sure where he came from. Not even the Sphinx knows. It's just one of those mysteries.
textByHashes.be10f8365d32d2e9d181264acb33ce630f35731c: Yuck! No, I would never debase myself to eat such filth.
textByHashes.6f2d83d6525bc8f49fd3f72a5c0e74e627510031: I am a cat of high breeding! My family tree dates back before those of any ruler on Gielinor.
textByHashes.353d2b0da37e1ce90c8c8ea27a2d8aaa62378eab: No, no, I just hunt them, play with them for a bit and then kill them.
textByHashes.d262916434c66cb3f5f1a75bb5f332c94dc3cdfd: What's evil about a little destruction?
textByHashes.7a9d44cf470da745203f5c963662df130eada9a5: Life can come from destruction too. Take a volcano. It will destroy all life that surrounds it, but it will also fertilise and enrich the surrounding earth.
textByHashes.8cbd316523c465c898ff602e4247724a9a17edf7: From that earth, more life will spring forth than there was in the first place.
textByHashes.9405fd310b4927cc9d09a448919f46d1bfb8cfad: I disagree. It's in my nature to prey on smaller life forms in the same way as it is in the volcanoes nature to explode.
textByHashes.a970c0cd328eea01bb28000321e36ef508744906: That is not too surprising. A long time ago, I served her. Her form has changed a lot since then, just as mine has.
textByHashes.f4055e6bf705066fa69eb24289d1722b5ed0f0e5: No, I used to be human. At the time when Amascut became the Devourer, I was one of her chief priestesses.
textByHashes.cc4ca8486cd99593023d921da94606813b6a7373: Not initially. Many of us tolerated her new habits for some time. She went too far though, so we took action.
textByHashes.a0f54507623777a3d6865c89611d793a92f1bb06: The last of her priestesses gathered and cursed her. Unfortunately, she also cast a spell of her own, condemning us to an eternity of walking Gielinor in her form.
textByHashes.6443a67b1f2d7d7b8abf5a84ea904d0a9a4d8104: She's as much of a cat as Icthlarin is a dog.
textByHashes.8e56964c796d6ae22acdef3d1dd59de046fea451: We trapped her in a human form, limiting her power. We also cursed her to never be willing worshipped again, though I fear that curse has started to wane.
textByHashes.3de9ac2b049b24eef220780b268ea9b3e991274c: Indeed. There are fewer of us priestesses left now, but we still hold enough power to be quite the problem for her.
textByHashes.c6f6e15e6c24919bc5168a2a6b664664a1782ec6: Not really. She is normally too wrapped up in preserving her immortality to be making conversation.
textByHashes.97fb2f44897f493ac75d54d6616bc253bfd1b0c2: Nothing of the sorts. I have nothing but respect for her.
textByHashes.2baf3d2b1d35cf07c508e0e9b7371dd57a2f365e: Well, because her immortality is based on the fact that she keeps us cats safe. Her whole existence is dedicated to protecting us.
textByHashes.676ff76eb37700f09264ce1cb97b54a72133bc35: True. Life shouldn't be hoarded, but lived. Of course, don't tell her I said that, otherwise I'd have to be on the lookout for a new guardian.
textByHashes.b9732991fd37fdf4f5c6fc306474e153bf6a3d31: Before she was the goddess of destruction, she was Amascut, the goddess of rebirth. Back then, I was human, and I served as one of her chief priestesses.
textByHashes.f4ddb33a3b544570be84cf354e14c6d317e513a5: When she became the Devourer, many of us tolerated her new habits for some time. After all, destruction was key to being the goddess of rebirth, so it was something we were comfortable with.
textByHashes.832b8a1d6a3fc765ff579404e38096d83f5231e1: Yes, so we took action. The last of her priestesses gathered and cursed her. Unfortunately, she also cast a spell of her own, condemning us to an eternity of walking Gielinor in her form.
textByHashes.d4aa5e01f2bb63426a1d187cad68235b9a08d2f0: Our curse trapped her in a human form, limiting her power. We also cursed her to never be willing worshipped again, though I fear that curse has started to wane.
textByHashes.b35b35a8ccbafe19caaf013a27c59a50b9fead1e: Out gallivanting. I think he's hunting noobs in the Wilderness this time. You know what he's like.
textByHashes.8e3ee8693bf0deb45c54f94042dd53e678a525f1: I like to roam as much as the next cat, but, every so often, I do like to come home.
